item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche announced that former Cuban leader Raúl Castro has been indicted over a 1996 incident in which Cuban jets shot down two civilian planes, killing four Cuban Americans.

media:content.media:title: Justice Department indicts former Cuban leader Raúl Castro
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche announced that former Cuban leader Raúl Castro has been indicted over a 1996 incident in which Cuban jets shot down two civilian planes, killing four Cuban Americans.

item.title: Justice Department indicts former Cuban leader Raúl Castro

item.description: The American doctor who tested positive for Ebola while working in Congo has been evacuated to Berlin to receive emergency care. Health officials are racing to trace infections in Central Africa and worry the scale of the outbreak is already larger than first reported. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Health Officials Race to Trace Rising Ebola Cases in Central Africa
media:content.media:description: The American doctor who tested positive for Ebola while working in Congo has been evacuated to Berlin to receive emergency care. Health officials are racing to trace infections in Central Africa and worry the scale of the outbreak is already larger than first reported. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

item.title: Health Officials Race to Trace Rising Ebola Cases in Central Africa

item.dc:creator: Erik Ortiz
item.description: An NBC News analysis of final statements from prisoners on Texas’ death row shows that some convey a readiness to die. Others are defiant. Many seek salvation.

media:content.media:text: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Jerome Butler, executed in 1990, was convicted in a 1986 shooting death.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Charlie Livingston, executed in 1997, was convicted in a 1983 robbery and murder.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Lawrence Buxton, executed in 1991, was convicted in a 1980 robbery and shooting death.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Warren Bridge, executed in 1994, was convicted in a 1980 robbery and shooting death.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

item.title: The haunting final words from Texas’ death row after five decades and 600 executions

item.description: Former Rep. Barney Frank, who was the first openly gay member of Congress and known for the Dodd-Frank Act, died at age 86. He had entered hospice care at his home in Maine in April and is survived by his husband of 14 years, Jim Ready.

media:content.media:title: Barney Frank, first openly gay member of Congress and Wall Street reformer, dies at 86
media:content.media:description: Former Rep. Barney Frank, who was the first openly gay member of Congress and known for the Dodd-Frank Act, died at age 86. He had entered hospice care at his home in Maine in April and is survived by his husband of 14 years, Jim Ready.

item.title: Barney Frank, first openly gay member of Congress and Wall Street reformer, dies at 86

item.description: Two officers who defended the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, filed a lawsuit to block the Trump administration's $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund. NBC News' Ryan Reilly reports.

media:content.media:title: Jan. 6 officers sue over Justice Department's $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund
media:content.media:description: Two officers who defended the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, filed a lawsuit to block the Trump administration's $1.8 billion "anti-weaponization" fund. NBC News' Ryan Reilly reports.

item.title: Jan. 6 officers sue over Justice Department's $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Four-time NFL Most Valuable Player Aaron Rodgers says he will retire after the upcoming season, ending a career that saw him become a fixture in the league for more than two decades

media:content.media:text: Image: Aaron Rodgers #8 of the Pittsburgh Steelers
media:content.media:description: Aaron Rodgers of the Pittsburgh Steelers takes the field prior to a game at Acrisure Stadium Pittsburgh in 2025.

media:content.media:text: Pittsburgh Steelers QB Aaron Rodgers throws a pass against the Baltimore Ravens.
media:content.media:description: Aaron Rodgers of the Pittsburgh Steelers takes the field prior to a game at Acrisure Stadium Pittsburgh in 2025.

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says he will retire after the 2026 NFL season: ‘This is it’

item.dc:creator: Gretchen Morgenson
item.description: Risks are mounting in the life insurance industry, and experts are concerned state regulators are not protecting policyholders.

media:content.media:text: A $100 dollar bill disappearing into red
media:content.media:description: The business of life insurance used to be boring. But the model has changed, with more aggressive insurers taking on more risks in their investments, research has shown.

media:content.media:text: Annie Benjamin
media:content.media:description: The business of life insurance used to be boring. But the model has changed, with more aggressive insurers taking on more risks in their investments, research has shown.

media:content.media:text: Image: Thomas D. Gober
media:content.media:description: The business of life insurance used to be boring. But the model has changed, with more aggressive insurers taking on more risks in their investments, research has shown.

item.title: She paid an insurance company $99,000 to generate retirement income for life. Then it collapsed.

item.description: NBC News projects that Virginia voters have approved redrawing the congressional district map. NBC News' Steve Kornacki provides analysis of the vote.

media:content.media:title: Virginia voters approve congressional redistricting amendment
media:content.media:description: NBC News projects that Virginia voters have approved redrawing the congressional district map. NBC News' Steve Kornacki provides analysis of the vote.

item.title: Virginia voters approve congressional redistricting amendment

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: David Ingram
item.description: SpaceX confirmed plans to sell shares of its stock to the public for the first time, according to a securities filing Wednesday outlining the company’s fundamentals

media:content.media:text: Elon Musk
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk in Paris in 2023.

media:content.media:text: Starship V3 on the launch pad during a wet dress rehearsal.
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk in Paris in 2023.

item.title: SpaceX confirms plans for an IPO that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
Pilar Melendez
item.description: The two teenage suspects in this week’s deadly attack on a San Diego mosque appear to have written a 75-page document replete with neo-Nazi ideology, incel rage and racist meme culture drawn from the darkest corners of the internet

media:content.media:text: Islamic center of San Diego active shooter
media:content.media:description: Multiple people were killed, including an armed security guard, after two shooters entered Islamic Center in San Diego Monday, May 18, 2026, and opened fire.

media:content.media:text: APTOPIX Islamic Center Shooting
media:content.media:description: A body is covered with a tarp at the scene of a shooting outside the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday.

media:content.media:text: US-RELIGION-CRIME
media:content.media:description: Two women cry as they leave a reunification center following the shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday.

media:content.media:text: People gather for a vigil the day after the shooting, outside of the Islamic Center of San Diego on Tuesday.
media:content.media:description: People gather for a vigil the day after the shooting, outside of the Islamic Center of San Diego on Tuesday.

item.title: San Diego mosque suspects’ writings reveal influence of online extremism, experts say

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: The president of Glendale Community College in Arizona was met with loud booing by students after she admitted the artificial intelligence that was being used to read names skipped over some graduates at the commencement ceremony last week.

media:content.media:text: The sign for Glendale Community College on a lawn outside
media:content.media:description: Glendale Community College in Glendale, Ariz.

item.title: Arizona college skips several graduates after an AI malfunction at commencement

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: A 12-year-old California girl who died in February after allegedly being struck in the head during what her family said was a bullying incident had a pre-existing medical condition that led to her death, according to the medical examiner.

media:content.media:text: Khimberly Zavaleta.
media:content.media:description: Khimberly Zavaleta.

item.title: Child who died after alleged bullying incident had pre-existing medical condition, medical examiner says

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: It’s last call for Schlitz, the beer that made Milwaukee famous.

media:content.media:text: A variety of antique Schlitz beer bottle are seen from the Leonard Jurgensen collection Wednesday, July 23, 2008, in Oconomowoc, Wis.
media:content.media:description: A variety of antique Schlitz beer bottles in Oconomowoc, Wisconsin.

media:content.media:text: Three apparently happy men enjoy their Schlitz beer, ca. 1920
media:content.media:description: A variety of antique Schlitz beer bottles in Oconomowoc, Wisconsin.

media:content.media:text: Queens, New York 1960 - Woodhaven Blvd Traffic & Billboard
media:content.media:description: A variety of antique Schlitz beer bottles in Oconomowoc, Wisconsin.

item.title: End of an era as Schlitz, the beer that made Milwaukee famous, pours its last

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Andrew Greif
Doha Madani
item.description: In Game 1 of the Eastern Conference finals, the New York Knicks trailed the Cleveland Cavaliers by 22 points in the fourth quarter last night with under eight minutes to play.

media:content.media:text: Jalen Brunson leaps in the air to make a basket
media:content.media:description: Jalen Brunson of the New York Knicks drives to the basket during the game against the Cleveland Cavaliers during Game One of the NBA Eastern Conference Finals, in New York City, on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Ben Stiller cheers in the crowd
media:content.media:description: Jalen Brunson of the New York Knicks drives to the basket during the game against the Cleveland Cavaliers during Game One of the NBA Eastern Conference Finals, in New York City, on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Taylor Hall prepares to hit a hockey puck on the ice
media:content.media:description: Jalen Brunson of the New York Knicks drives to the basket during the game against the Cleveland Cavaliers during Game One of the NBA Eastern Conference Finals, in New York City, on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Mo Salah, center, kicks the soccer ball while in between two players from the opposing team
media:content.media:description: Jalen Brunson of the New York Knicks drives to the basket during the game against the Cleveland Cavaliers during Game One of the NBA Eastern Conference Finals, in New York City, on Tuesday.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Knicks mount epic fourth quarter comeback, stun Cavaliers

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Four-time NFL Most Valuable Player Aaron Rodgers says he will retire after the upcoming season, ending a career that saw him become a fixture in the league for more than two decades

media:content.media:description: Aaron Rodgers of the Pittsburgh Steelers takes the field prior to a game at Acrisure Stadium Pittsburgh in 2025.

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says he will retire after the 2026 NFL season: ‘This is it’

item.dc:creator: Reuters
item.description: Federal Reserve officials’ concerns about inflation being stoked by the Iran war intensified last month.

media:content.media:text: Federal Reserve To Make Latest Interest Rate Announcement Wednesday
media:content.media:description: Marriner S. Eccles Federal Reserve Board Building in in Washington, D.C.

media:content.media:text: Kevin Warsh.
media:content.media:description: Marriner S. Eccles Federal Reserve Board Building in in Washington, D.C.

item.title: Fed officials’ concerns about inflation sparked talk of rate hike during last meeting

item.dc:creator: Erik Ortiz
item.description: An NBC News analysis of final statements from prisoners on Texas’ death row shows that some convey a readiness to die. Others are defiant. Many seek salvation.

media:content.media:text: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.
media:content.media:description: The last words of Texas' executed inmates are transcribed by hand and collected online.

media:content.media:text: Jerome Butler, executed in 1990, was convicted in a 1986 shooting death.

media:content.media:text: Lawrence Buxton, executed in 1991, was convicted in a 1980 robbery and shooting death.

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
Carmen Sesin
Ryan J. Reilly
Gary Grumbach
Orlando Matos
item.description: The Justice Department indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on Wednesday in connection to the 1996 shooting of two civilian planes that killed four Cuban Americans.

media:content.media:text: Raul Castro
media:content.media:description: Raul Castro in Havana on May 1, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Photos of Brothers to the Rescue pilots Carlos Costa, Amando Alejandre Jr., Mario de la Peña and Pablo Morales.
media:content.media:description: Photos of Brothers to the Rescue pilots Carlos Costa, Amando Alejandre Jr., Mario de la Peña and Pablo Morales displayed at Florida International University.

media:content.media:text: Cuba Brothers to the Rescue.
media:content.media:description: Some of the boats from the flotilla head for open waters in 1996 off Key West, Fla., carrying Cuban Americans to the site of the downing of two Brothers to the Rescue planes by Cuban fighters.

item.title: DOJ indicts Raúl Castro over fatal 1996 civilian planes’ shooting

item.dc:creator: Berkeley Lovelace Jr.
item.description: A vaccine for the Ebola strain driving the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is likely months away from human trials, and there is no guarantee it would work, the World Health Organization said Wednesday.

media:content.media:text: A staff member of the CBCA Virunga Hospital checks a visitor's temperature
media:content.media:description: A staff member of the CBCA Virunga Hospital in Goma, Congo, checks a visitor's temperature.

item.title: Ebola vaccine for Bundibugyo strain could take months before human trials

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Amazon Executive Chairman Jeff Bezos accused politicians of villainizing the ultra-wealthy and using tax policy as a political wedge issue.

media:content.media:text: Jeff Bezos.
media:content.media:description: Jeff Bezos, founder of Amazon.com Inc., in Miami in 2025.

item.title: Jeff Bezos says raising taxes on the wealthy won’t help the average American

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against The Walt Disney Co.

media:content.media:text: Anaheim Exteriors And Landmarks - 2023
media:content.media:description: Disneyland in Anaheim, Calif. in 2023.

item.title: Disney sued over facial recognition technology at California parks

item.dc:creator: Matt Lavietes
item.description: A TikTok star was arrested in California this week and accused of trying to hire someone to kill her daughter’s father, boy band singer Jack Avery

media:content.media:text: Infulencer Gabbie Gonazales; singer Jack Avery.
media:content.media:description: Jack Avery of Why Don't We performs in 2019.

media:content.media:text: Influencer Gabbie Gonazalez; singer Jack Avery.
media:content.media:description: Jack Avery of Why Don't We performs in 2019.

media:content.media:text: 2022 iHeartRadio Music Awards - Arrivals
media:content.media:description: Jack Avery of Why Don't We performs in 2019.

item.title: A TikTok star recruited her dad to kill her boy band baby daddy, officials say

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Wednesday is another sweltering day for those in the mid-Atlantic as temperatures soar into the mid-90s once again just before a storm front is set to take over the Eastern Seaboard

media:content.media:text: APTOPIX US Severe Weather New York
media:content.media:description: A woman uses a fan in Times Square during a heat advisory in New York, Tuesday, May 19, 2026.

media:content.media:text: California Wildfires
media:content.media:description: A firefighter works to control the Sandy Fire in Simi Valley, Calif., on Tuesday.

item.title: Extreme heat lingers across the mid-Atlantic, but storms are expected to cool off states

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: Jan. 6 officers sue over $1.8B pot they call ‘slush fund’ for ‘insurrectionists’

media:content.media:text: Daniel Hodges, MPD officer, left, and Harry Dunn, U.S. Capitol Police officer, listen during a hearing on the Jan. 6 attack at the Capitol on June 21, 2022.
media:content.media:description: Daniel Hodges, MPD officer, left, and Harry Dunn, U.S. Capitol Police officer, listen during a Jan. 6 committee hearing at the Capitol on June 21, 2022.

media:content.media:text: Rioters and police inside the Capitol
media:content.media:description: Trump supporters clash with police inside the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: Jan. 6 officers sue over $1.8B pot they call ‘slush fund’ for ‘insurrectionists’
